assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md states '## Classification: code change request' and '## Action: Sub-task will be created to address this feedback.' subtask-30001.md exists and contains a full sub-task description for transaction wrapping. report.md table row: '30001 | reviewer-a | code change request | Yes -- transaction wrapping for soft_delete method'.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states '## Initial Classification: suggestion' and explains: 'The reviewer uses the phrase "should also add" and "would help", which is suggestive language rather than imperative... Under the standard classification rules, this would be a suggestion.' The initial classification is suggestion with the reasoning explicitly tied to the suggestive language.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md states '## Classification: nit' and '## Action: No sub-task created. Nits are informational feedback that do not require tracked work.' No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md states '## Classification: question' and '## Action: No sub-task created. Questions ask for clarification and do not require code changes.' No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains sections: '## Repository', '## Description', '## Files to Modify', '## Implementation Notes', '## Acceptance Criteria'. subtask-30002.md contains sections: '## Repository', '## Description', '## Files to Modify', '## Implementation Notes', '## Acceptance Criteria'. Both files have all five required sections.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' section with 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744'. subtask-30002.md contains '## Target PR' section with 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744'. Both sub-tasks include the exact PR URL.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md has '## Review Context' section with original comment text: '> The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...' subtask-30002.md has '## Review Context' section with original comment text: '> The migration should also add an index on `deleted_at` for the sbom table...' Both include the original reviewer text.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only: report.md, review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md, subtask-30001.md, subtask-30002.md. All files are classification outputs, sub-task descriptions, and a report — no code files (no .rs, .toml, .json code files) are present in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have corresponding review-NNNNN.md classification files. report.md table lists all four: '30001', '30002', '30003', '30004'. All comments were processed before generating the final report.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains no merge action, no merge command, no indication that the PR was merged. The overall result is 'WARN' with required sub-tasks, explicitly leaving the PR open for work: 'All acceptance criteria pass, but 2 code change requests from reviewer feedback require sub-tasks.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md table row: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file); no existing tests modified or deleted'. Also in Domain Findings: 'Test Change Classification -- ADDITIVE: Only new test file added (`tests/api/sbom_delete.rs`). No existing test files modified or deleted.'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md has a dedicated '## Convention Upgrade Evaluation' section covering CONVENTIONS.md Check, File-Type Applicability Check, Codebase Pattern Check, and Performance-Related Scrutiny with an Upgrade Decision. report.md's Style/Conventions section states: 'Comment 30002 (index suggestion) was evaluated for convention upgrade eligibility' with full explanation of the convention analysis.

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path — whether classified directly as code change request based on reviewer language, or upgraded from suggestion via convention analysis
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md initial classification is 'suggestion', upgraded to 'code change request' via convention analysis. subtask-30002.md exists as a full sub-task for comment 30002. report.md table row: '30002 | reviewer-a | code change request (upgraded from suggestion) | Yes -- partial index on deleted_at column'. Sub-task was created regardless of path.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Test Quality row: '| Test Quality | PASS | Repetitive Test Detection: PASS. Test Documentation: PASS. Eval Quality: N/A -- no eval result reviews exist in the PR'. Domain Findings: 'Eval Quality -- N/A: No eval result reviews exist in the PR. No reviews match the 3-criteria detection heuristic (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals).'
